title: CloudFront Origins, OAC, SSE-KMS, and Private Delivery · Execution Trace: Oac Kms Policy
summary: A distribution points at an S3 website endpoint, retains legacy OAI by habit, permits the CloudFront service principal globally, and forgets the KMS key policy. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the oac kms policy increment in private CloudFront-to-S3 origin contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Grant the exact distribution use of the KMS key separately from S3 bucket access and verify encrypted read and write cases.
Scope the CloudFront service principal to the exact distribution SourceArn and required object actions without public or cross-distribution access.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Oac Kms Policy · Theory Oac Kms Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 7833 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a regular S3 OAC origin with exact distribution and KMS authorization; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Grant the exact distribution use of the KMS key separately from S3 bucket access and verify encrypted read and write cases.
